Reported aggregate accuracy. Some agents have null when only an upper bound was reported.

Free-text note on caveats per row.

tables.name: per_task
tables.note: BrowseComp does not publish per-task or per-run results. The benchmark dataset (1,266 problems) is in openai/simple-evals on GitHub, but task-level grading outputs from the original paper are not released. The Kaggle leaderboard exposes submission accuracies but no per-task download. Gate 1 (run-level data) FAILS for this candidate.

structural_findings.no_run_level_public_data: BrowseComp's published artifacts cover the dataset (questions and answers) and aggregate accuracies for the agents OpenAI evaluated in the launch paper. There is no public run-level data, no per-task grading record, and no token/cost telemetry. This makes it unusable as GAIA HAL Generalist under the v0 selection gates.
structural_findings.binary_outcome_with_no_uncertainty: Reported accuracies in the launch paper are single point estimates with no confidence intervals, no seed information, and no per-task indicators — exactly the methodology gap the eval-audit project is meant to address. BrowseComp is therefore a useful future target for reanalysis IF run-level data is ever released, but not for v0.
